provisions.title: Citation and commencement
provisions.content: These By-laws may be cited as the Moshi Municipal Council (De­velopment Levy ) By-laws, 2002 and shall be deemed to have come into operation on the 1 st day of January 2002.

provisions.title: Application
provisions.content: These By-laws shall apply to the whole of the area of Jurisdiction of the Moshi Municipal Council.

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In this By-laws unless the context otherwise requires:— " Act " means the Local Government Finances Act, 1982; " council " means the Moshi Municipal Council or an agent duly authorised to administer these By-laws; " Director " means the Moshi Municipal Director who is responsible for he management and control of the day-to-day affairs of the Council; " income " means the annual earnings from the remuneration, business or any other sources. [No. 9 of 1982] " Levy " means the annual rate designated as "Development Levy" made by the Council and payable under the provisions of these By-laws and in accordance with the Provisions of the Act ; " Authorised officer " means any person appointed to administer these By­laws and includes an appointed agent; " Levy collector " means any person appointed as such under these By­laws; " Low income earners " means any person with an annual income of two hundred thousand shillings. " Minister " means the Minister responsible for Regional Administration and Local Governments; " Resident " means any entity or individual person of or above the age of eighteen years who habitually resides and or works for gains, and upon whom the power of the council , is exercisable; " Year of income " means a period in which the levy is payable; [ Cap 366 ] " Employee " means and includes a person defined to be an employee under the Employment Ordinance; " Employer " means any person or any firm, corporation or Company, public authority or body of persons who or which has entered into a contract of service to employ any person and includes any agent, foreman, manager and any officer who is acting on behalf of the government. " Businessman " means and includes any person or group of persons or corporate entity carrying on business or any activity for gain under any written Laws of By-laws in the area of jurisdiction of the Council.

provisions.title: Payment of levy
provisions.content: With effect from the 1 st day of January, 2002, every resident within the Moshi Municipality who is liable to pay Levy , shall pay such levy at the rates as specified in the schedule to these By-laws.

provisions.title: Graduated and uniform rate systems
provisions.content: The levy payable under these By-laws shall be based upon gradu­ated and uniforms rate systems.

provisions.title: Furnishing information and remuneration
provisions.content: The Director may notice in writing require any employer or any other person responsible for the payment of remuneration to furnish him within a reasonable time, not being more than thirty days from the date of that notice, with a return containing the names and addresses of all persons to whom payments and allowances are made by the employer in respect of their employment, and the amounts of payments and allow­ances made to each of such persons, and any other particulars deemed to be necessary for the ascertainment of the income of such person.

provisions.title: Business to pay in accordance with the business licence fee
provisions.content: (1) It shall be a duty of every businessman to pay the levy due to him in accordance with an amount assessed for his business-licensing fee liability payable to the Council or other licensing authority. (2) The so deducted amount shall be deemed to be an income gained by such businessman in the year.

provisions.title: Where an employee is a business
provisions.content: Where it appears that a levy payer is also an employee and at the same time is a businessman; it shall be a duty of such person: (i) To pay through his employer the levy due from his wages, and (ii) to pay the levy due to the Council in accordance with his busi­ness licensing fee liability.

provisions.title: Failure to deduct
provisions.content: If an employer required to deduct and remit a Levy from his employee fails, refuses or neglect to do so in accordance with the notice under which the deduction are made, he shall himself be liable for the amount and the Director may appoint an agent or Court Broker to distrain his movable property of the equal value at the employers risks and or institute a summary Proceeding in order to recover the amount as a debt to the Council together with full costs of distaining or suit.

provisions.title: Power to enter
provisions.content: The Director may direct any authorised officer to enter into any premises of any employer or any other levy collector or levy payer for the purposes of conducting an inquiry and obtaining such information or particulars for the purposes of collecting levy or ascertainment of the income liable to the levy payer.

provisions.title: Assessment by the director
provisions.content: Where an authorised officer is unable to ascertain a true and correct income of the resident for the purposes of assessing the levy, the Director may to the best of his judgement determine any assessment of an individual other than low-income earner or a peasant provided that he may formulate "Standard assessment" of an individual person or group of persons other than the levy payable by a low income earner or peas­ant, taking into account the income and circumstances of such individual person or group of persons.

provisions.title: Power to appoint levy collector
provisions.content: (1) The Director may appoint any suitable person to collect and remit the levy to the Council and that person shall have the responsibil­ity to carry out such duties as prescribed under these By-laws. (2) For the purpose of efficient administration of the levy, every em­ployer is hereby appointed to be a levy collector and it shall be the duty of every employer:— (a) to deduct the amount due from the remuneration of employee and (b) to remit such levy to the Council within the due dates specified in the notice. Provided that the Director shall prescribe the manner of deducting the levy from the remunerations of the employee. (3) The Director may by notice in writing require any person who holds money on behalf of the resident or person deemed to be a resident under these By-laws to utilise such money within such period as speci­fied in the notice for the liquidation of the levy by paying such sums of money to the Council.

(1) Any employer required to deduct the levy under these By­laws shall deduct such amount and remit the levy paid to the Director in three equal instalment in the months between February, and August of each Year. (2) The amount so deducted shall be remitted to the Director not later than ten days of the month proceeding the month in respect of which the deduction was made.

provisions.title: Failure to remit the levy
provisions.content: Where an employer or any levy collector appointed under these By-laws fails to remit the amount of levy payable after the due date the employer or levy collector shall be deemed to be the resident in respect of all employees or other person and such amount so payable shall become a public debt and any provisions relating to the collection and recovery of the levy shall apply to him as if it were a levy payable by the employer or any other person and the employer or levy collector commits an offence.

provisions.title: Due date
provisions.content: (1) The 31st day of August of every year of income shall be the last day on which a resident or may employee or businessman shall have paid the levy due to the Council. (2) Where the levy remains unpaid after the due date, the resident or collector commits an offence or shall be subject to recovery measures under these By-laws.

provisions.title: Surcharge on unpaid levy
provisions.content: Where after the 31 st day of August of every year, the levy re­mains unpaid by the resident such person shall in addition to the levy payable, have to pay surcharge equal of fifty per centum of the levy payable by him.

provisions.title: Exemption from and remission of rates
provisions.content: The Council may from time to time by a duly convened meeting, exempt One of or category of the following group of person from pay­ing the levy wholly or part of it. (a) Person who shall produce to Director of levy Collector appointed by the Council a valid receipt issued by the other Council evi­dencing payment by him of the rate levied by such Council for the current year except to the extend, if any, by which the rate exceeds that to which the receipt relates. (b) Any person who is completely dependent on others for his live­lihood. (c) Women who are completely dependent on their husbands and of have no visible source of income . (d) Full-time students of the age of or above eighteen years who for inability to work, depends on others for their live hood. (e) Disabled persons who have no visible sources of income . (f) Any person, or other Category or person whom the Council thinks fit, for any reason, that they should be exempted from paying the levy.

provisions.title: Duty to register
provisions.content: It shall be a duty of every resident to register his name in the Levy Log at the place and in the manner as shall be directed by the Director from time to time.

provisions.title: Offences
provisions.content: (1) Any person, group of persons or levy collector who without reasonable Cause:— (a) Fails to furnish a return required under these By-laws; (b) Fails to remit the levy after the due date; (c) Fails to keep any records or information or pertaining to the income chargeable to the levy; (d) Fails to produce any information or particulars for examination in accordance with these By-laws; (e) Wilfully neglects with intent to escape from Liability, fails to register or incites others not to register their names in the Levy Log; (f) Obstruct or attempts to obstruct any authorised officer in per­formance of his duties or in the exercise of the power conferred on him under these By-laws commit an offence and upon convic­tion he shall be liable to a fine of fifty thousand shillings for each offence or imprisonment for twelve months for each offence or to both such fine and imprisonment; (2) The Council may demand from such person or group of person to pay an amount deemed to be a loss of gain caused by the act of such person or group of persons by using any lawful means. Provided that such amount so demanded shall not exceed the actual amount lost due to the accused's conduct plus a penalty of fifty per centum of such amount and the costs of recovery.

provisions.title: Power of the Director to compound offence
provisions.content: Where any person commits any offence under these by-laws the Director may compound such offence and orders that person to pay such amount of levy as he thinks fit provided that such amount should not be below twenty thousand shillings for each offence.

provisions.title: Repeal
provisions.content: The Moshi Municipal Council (Development Levy ) By-laws, 1997 and any other By-laws which may have been deemed to be existing for the Development levy are hereby repealed. [GN. No 667 of 1997] Schedule No. Qualifying person Rate payable 1. Employees and low income earners 3000/= Three thousand shillings per annum 2. Business men Ten per centllm of the business-licensing fee.
